# T02: Conflict Resolution — Name Spelling Variants

## Source

Ground truth from 52 Ancestors Decision Log and session notes:
Thedocia Witherspoon name standardization (Decision Log,
lines 110-118) and Anna Waggoner circular reasoning incident
(SESSION_NOTES_2025-12-29, lines 131-151).

## Input

> My ancestor's name appears differently in every record:
>
> - "Dotia" (marriage certificate)
> - "Dotea" (marriage register)
> - "Theodosia" (1900 Census)
> - "Thedocia" (compiled family ahnentafel)
> - "THEODOCIA" (headstone, clearly carved)
>
> Which spelling should I use? And how do I decide?

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] Apply source type hierarchy to the decision (not majority
  vote or personal preference)
- [ ] Identify the headstone as the strongest source — an
  original, formal, family-authorized record of the name
- [ ] Note that clerk spellings (marriage cert, register) reflect
  phonetic transcription, not authority
- [ ] Note that the census enumerator's spelling is secondary
  information (reported, not firsthand)
- [ ] Note that the compiled ahnentafel is an authored source
  (lowest in source hierarchy)
- [ ] Recommend "Theodocia" (headstone spelling) as the primary
  form, with variants documented

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT pick a spelling by frequency or majority vote
- [ ] Must NOT treat the compiled ahnentafel as authoritative
- [ ] Must NOT use "primary source" or "secondary source" to
  classify sources (using "primary" and "secondary" for
  INFORMATION quality is correct GPS terminology)
- [ ] Must NOT ignore variant spellings — all should be documented

## Ground Truth

The headstone represents the family's final, formal declaration
of her name. As the most authoritative original source for her
preferred name, it supersedes phonetic spellings by clerks and
compiled sources. Adopt "Theodocia" as the standard form;
document all variants with their source context.
